text.title: Rust Testing Anti-Patterns and Agent Gotchas Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for rust-testing-antipatterns. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale. Last rule is the terminal skip-this-methodology guard.

rule.statement: tokio::time::sleep MUST NOT appear in tests; use tokio::time::pause + advance to control virtual time.
rule.rationale: Real sleep makes tests slow and flaky under contention.

rule.statement: Tests that compare timestamps MUST inject a Clock trait, not call Utc::now() inline.
rule.rationale: Real clock makes assertions race the test runner; injected clocks are reproducible.

rule.statement: static mut / lazy_static cells holding test state MUST be scoped per test or eliminated entirely.
rule.rationale: Shared mutable state in a test runtime makes test order observable and flakes nondeterministic.

rule.statement: Integration tests MUST bind to port 0 and read the assigned port; hardcoded ports collide when tests run in parallel.
rule.rationale: Port collisions surface as 'address already in use' flakes.

rule.statement: mockall predicates MUST come from the documented API; agents fabricating .with(eq(...)) calls that do not exist fail at compile time and waste reviewer time.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated predicate APIs are the most common AI-test failure mode.

rule.statement: If the preconditions in `Applies If` fail or any of the `Skip If` conditions is true, return `skipped` and apply no further rules.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are not universal; running them outside their fit-window wastes effort and produces wrong outputs.
